original_abstract: While deep learning has catalyzed transformative breakthroughs across computer vision, natural language processing, and information retrieval, these advancements have come at a significant cost. As models have grown in sophistication, so too have their parameter counts, inference latencies, and computational requirements. This evolution necessitates a paradigm shift: researchers must now prioritize a model's operational footprint as rigorously as its predictive quality. This paper formalizes the challenges of efficiency in deep learning and provides a comprehensive survey of five core domains—ranging from algorithmic modeling to infrastructure and hardware acceleration. In addition to reviewing seminal literature, we offer a practical, experiment-driven guide and accompanying codebase to assist practitioners in optimizing training and deployment workflows. To our knowledge, this is the first survey to synthesize the entire landscape of model efficiency across the full stack. By providing both a conceptual framework and actionable techniques, we aim to empower researchers and engineers to achieve immediate performance gains while fostering new avenues for experimentation in sustainable AI.
humanized_abstract: Deep learning has revolutionized the field of computer vision, natural language processing and information retrieval. While achieving many groundbreaking successes, deep learning models have come at the cost of efficiency. As accuracy has improved, model size has increased, and computation time and memory required to run models have also dramatically increased. As such, model efficiency must be given the same importance as model accuracy, and the challenges and techniques surrounding this new goal are not yet well understood. In this paper, we formally introduce the challenges surrounding model efficiency and give a survey of five areas surrounding model efficiency, from neural network design and training methods, to compilers, runtimes, and hardware acceleration. This paper reviews prior work in the field, and provides the reader with a guide and set of experiments with open source code to help the reader quickly implement a set of techniques to improve their training and inference time. We aim to be the first comprehensive survey of model efficiency from start to finish, while also serving as an impetus for advancing research in the field of high efficiency AI, while at the same time allowing researchers and practitioners to immediately gain tangible performance improvements.
